name: Hydrogen Peroxide (3%)
description: A versatile oxidizing agent that breaks down to water and oxygen, used as an EPA-registered disinfectant, oxygen bleach, and oral care agent.
appearance: Colorless liquid (3% solution appears identical to water)
solubility: Miscible with water in all proportions; soluble in ethanol
chemistry: Oxidizer destroying bacterial membranes. EPA-registered disinfectant. Breaks down to water + oxygen. Bleaches via oxidation.
warnings: Degrades in light. Don't mix with vinegar. Can bleach fabrics.
